channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Grammy Awards: How to watch the ceremony, red carpet arrivals, interviews
item.description: The 67th annual Grammy Awards are set to recognize some of the most talented performers and biggest hits in pop music on Sunday, with different ways to watch the festivities.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: How to save money on your taxes
item.description: You may qualify for tax credits for caring for a child or dependent or investing in clean energy for your home.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, Fox 5

item.title: Trump says Americans could feel 'some pain' from his new tariffs that are triggering a trade war
item.description: President Donald Trump says Americans could feel “some pain” from the emerging trade war triggered by his tariffs against Canada, Mexico and China.
item.dc:creator: Josh Boak, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: China to file lawsuit with WTO, vows 'countermeasures' in response to Trump tariffs
item.description: In a statement, the ministry’s spokesperson vowed further “countermeasures” against the United States but did not specify what steps it would take.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Trump's second week in office delivers jolts and chaotic orders with a mix of politics and tragedy
item.description: President Donald Trump's second week in office seemed to deliver a daily dose of deliberate jolts for the country. There were chaotic reminders of his first term. The White House found itself backtracking on its directive to freeze federal spending on grants and loans.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, CHRIS MEGERIAN and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump puts tariffs on Canada, Mexico and China, spurring trade war as North American allies respond
item.description: President Donald Trump on Saturday signed an order to impose stiff tariffs on imports from Mexico, Canada and China, drawing swift retaliation and an undeniable sense of betrayal from the country's North American neighbors as a trade war erupted among the longtime allies.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, ZEKE MILLER, ROB GILLIES and CHRISTOPHER SHERMAN, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Stunning trade sends Luka Doncic to Lakers, Anthony Davis to Mavericks
item.description: In what is one of the more surreal trades in the history of professional sports, the Los Angeles Lakers have sent away one of its franchise legends in exchange for a young superstar.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KTLA 5

item.title: A 2,500th 3-pointer, a triple-double, Bronny's basket give LeBron James another memorable MSG game
item.description: A 2,500th 3-pointer for his career and his 10th triple-double this season. Not a bad 31st trip to Madison Square Garden for LeBron James. Pretty good first one for Bronny James, too.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MAHONEY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump signs tariffs on Canada, Mexico and China
item.description: "Tariffs on imports from Canada, Mexico, and China are SIGNED!" White House spokesperson Harrison Fields posted on the social media platform X.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Minneapolis will pay $600,000 to settle with woman who says ex-Officer Chauvin knelt on her neck
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — The city of Minneapolis has agreed to pay $600,000 to settle a lawsuit by a woman who alleged that ex-Officer Derek Chauvin hauled her from her minivan and pinned her to the ground with his knee in January 2020, just as he did four months later when he killed George Floyd.
item.dc:creator: STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Ken Martin elected next DNC chair
item.description: Minnesota state party chair Ken Martin was elected on Saturday as the next leader of the Democratic National Committee (DNC) after securing a majority of the 448 DNC committee members’ votes.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, WFLA, NBC

item.title: ICE crackdown sees 7,400 illegal migrants arrested in 9 days
item.description: Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) has arrested more than 7,400 people in nine days across several states amid its aggressive crackdown on illegal immigration since the new Trump administration came into office.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A list of government web pages that have gone dark to comply with Trump orders
item.description: A number of U.S. government web pages changed or went dark Friday as agencies scrambled to comply with President Donald Trump's executive orders declaring his administration would recognize only two genders and ordering an end to diversity, equity and inclusion initiatives.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN J. COOPER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Medical plane carrying pediatric patient crashes in Philadelphia, sets homes ablaze
item.description: A medical transport jet headed for Missouri and carrying two people and four crew members crashed in Northeast Philadelphia Friday night.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, Fox 5

item.title: What to know about the NTSB, the agency investigating the DC plane crash
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A collision between an American Airlines passenger jet and an Army helicopter near Washington, D.C., that killed 67 people brought renewed focus on the federal agency charged with investigating aviation disasters.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: It costs the most to rent an apartment in these US cities: report
item.description: The price of renting an apartment is on the rise, particularly in some major U.S. cities. This is according to the latest rent report from Zumper, which has found that across the nation, the price for a one-bedroom apartment is up 2.5% since this time last year.
item.dc:creator: Nick Jachim, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Soldiers in deadly helicopter crash with DC plane identified
item.description: The U.S. Army has identified two of the three soldiers who were killed Wednesday night when their helicopter crashed into an airplane in D.C.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Robert Santos decides to resign as US Census Bureau director midway through a 5-year term
item.description: Robert Santos, who emphasized inclusivity and outreach to overlooked communities, has decided to resign as director of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MIKE SCHNEIDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Department of Education undoes Biden's Title IX rules on gender
item.description: The Department of Education announced Friday it is scrapping former President Biden's 2024 Title IX changes, getting rid of discrimination protection based on gender identity and sexual orientation and changing how sexual assault cases are handled on campus.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WGN 9

item.title: ICT Together fund will help crash victims' families
item.description: The Wichita Foundation has started a fund to provide aid to help families of the American Airlines crash victims.
item.dc:creator: Laura McMillan, NewsNation

item.title: Butler Community College instructor killed in DC plane crash
item.description: The National Association of Biology Teachers has confirmed another victim of the Wichita plane crash in Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: Carina Branson, NewsNation

item.title: Officials probe cause of DC plane crash
item.description: American Airlines Flight 5342 with 60 passengers and four crew hit an Army helicopter near Washington D.C.'s Reagan National Wednesday night, sending the two aircraft into the Potomac River and killing all aboard in the deadliest U.S. air crash in more than two decades.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS and HALLIE GOLDEN Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: DC plane crash: Recovery continues, NTSB says investigation ongoing
item.description: Air crash investigations can take months, and federal investigators told reporters they would not speculate on the cause.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: San Diego among most expensive cities to rent a one-bedroom apartment: report
item.description: The price of renting an apartment is on the rise, particularly in some major U.S. cities, including San Diego.
item.dc:creator: Nick Jachim, Fox 5

item.title: Congress authorized more flights at Reagan National despite warnings
item.description: Congress added more flights to Reagan National's schedule last year despite warnings from the airport.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, KRON4

item.title: FireAid delivered loads of surprises. Here are some of the best moments from the musical benefit
item.description: INGLEWOOD, Calif. (AP) — The FireAid benefit was fueled by some of music’s best performers to raise money for Los Angeles-area wildfire relief efforts.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN LANDRUM Jr. and MARK KENNEDY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Kash Patel grilled on potential Trump pressure, past comments at contentious hearing
item.description: Kash Patel, President Trump’s nominee to lead the FBI, was at the center of a contentious confirmation hearing Thursday, facing intense grilling from Democrats on the Senate Judiciary Committee about his past comments and allegiance to Trump.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, NewsNation

item.title: Civil rights attorney from Wichita, Kansas, victim of plane crash in DC
item.description: A woman who made a name for herself in Wichita and was in the process of doing the same thing in Washington was on the plane that crashed in Washington, D.C., on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Payton Steiner, NewsNation

item.title: Pioneering Black Army general who lived in El Paso dies at 100
item.description: Retired Army Maj. Gen. Edward Greer, who broke racial barriers during his long Army career, died at age 100 at his El Paso home on Wednesday, Jan. 29, his family said.
item.dc:creator: Dave Burge, NewsNation

item.title: Mushers and dogs braved a horrific Alaska winter to deliver lifesaving serum 100 years ago
item.description: ANCHORAGE, Alaska (AP) — The Alaska Gold Rush town of Nome faced a bleak winter. It was hundreds of miles from anywhere, cut off by the frozen sea and unrelenting blizzards, and under siege from a contagious disease known as the “strangling angel” for the way it suffocated children.
item.dc:creator: MARK THIESSEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Midair collision kills 67 people in the deadliest US air disaster in almost a quarter century
item.description: ARLINGTON, Va. (AP) — A midair collision between an Army helicopter and a jetliner killed all 67 people aboard the two aircraft, officials said Thursday, as they scrutinized the actions of the military pilot in the country's deadliest aviation disaster in almost a quarter century.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, TARA COPP, ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON and LEA SKENE, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Marianne Faithfull, singer, actress and Rolling Stones muse, dies at 78
item.description: The 1960s icon passed in London surrounded by family, according to a statement from her record promotion group.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Judge extends court-monitoring agreement for children in Customs and Border Protection custody
item.description: McAllen, Texas (AP) — A federal judge extended a court agreement on Thursday ensuring safe and sanitary conditions for migrant children in federal custody a day after U.S. Customs and Border Protection was set to begin self-monitoring.
item.dc:creator: VALERIE GONZALEZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Engineering student among DC plane crash victims
item.description: DAYTON, Ohio (WDTN) -- Cedarville University is grieving one of its own after the devastating plane crash in Washington D.C. Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Emily Lewis, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Republicans leave Florida frustrated with lack of final plan to pass Trump agenda
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) arrived at this week’s House Republican retreat with hopes of uniting the fractious GOP conference around a plan to pass President Trump's agenda — but instead, the group is departing south Florida with rising tensions in its ranks. Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene (R-Ga.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Hawaii wildfire victims spared from testifying after last-minute deal over $4B settlement
item.description: HONOLULU (AP) — Lawyers representing victims of a deadly Hawaii wildfire reached a last-minute deal averting a trial that was scheduled to begin Wednesday over how to split a $4 billion settlement.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER SINCO KELLEHER, Associated Press, ABC 4
